LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SUBARU OF AMERICA, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT AS TO THE ALLEGATIONS REGARDING RECURRENT EXCESSIVE OIL CONSUMPTION IS GRANTED. THE CLASS ACTION SETTLEMENT IN YAEGER IS BINDING ON PLAINTIFFS NATHANIEL BUTLIN AND STACEY SHROUT AND IS NOT AN IMPERMISSIBLE WAIVER OF THEIR RIGHTS UNDER THE SONG-BEVERLY ACT. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG; DEPT. 302.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-08-2017 Vacated

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-27-2017 Set For Trial On Nov-13-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SUBARU OF AMERICA, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT as to the allegations regarding recurrent excessive oil consumption is granted. The class action settlement in Yaeger is binding on plaintiffs Nathaniel Butlin and Stacey Shrout and is not an impermissible waiver of their rights under the Song-Beverly Act. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
